question: What are the hostel fee details at JECRC University?
answer: JECRC University hostel fees for 2026-27: AC rooms cost Rs. 1,72,000 per year (paid in 2 installments: Rs. 1,03,000 first + Rs. 69,000 second). Non-AC rooms cost Rs. 1,27,000 per year (Rs. 76,000 first + Rs. 51,000 second). There is also a one-time non-refundable registration fee of Rs. 5,000. Gymnasium is optional at Rs. 1,000 per semester. GST applicable extra. Note: There are NO single occupancy rooms — rooms are apartment-style with 3 students sharing a flat (each gets a private bedroom + shared hall and bathroom).

question: Does JECRC have AC and non-AC hostel rooms?
answer: Yes. JECRC University offers both AC and Non-AC hostel rooms. AC rooms cost Rs. 1,72,000/year and Non-AC rooms cost Rs. 1,27,000/year (2026-27 rates). All rooms are apartment-style — 3 students share a flat with individual private bedrooms, a common hall, and a shared bathroom. There are no single occupancy rooms. Separate hostels for boys and girls. Girls gate closes at 9 PM, boys at 10 PM.

question: What is the hostel timing and rules at JECRC?
answer: JECRC hostel gate closes at 9:00 PM for girls and 10:00 PM for boys. Daily attendance is taken by the warden at a specified time — students must be present in their rooms. Prohibited items include drugs, liquor, weapons, high-powered electrical appliances (iron, induction cooker, hot water rod). No smoking or consumption of alcohol on premises. Hostel fee once paid is non-refundable. Fee covers 10 months; extra charges apply for summer stay.

question: What is the hostel facility like at JECRC University?
answer: JECRC University has 3 boys hostels on campus and 1 girls hostel on campus (plus additional approved hostels nearby). Total capacity is 1000-1500 students. Rooms are apartment-style — 3 students per flat, each with a private bedroom, shared hall, and shared bathroom. Facilities include: 24/7 security, CCTV, Wi-Fi (though speed has been reported as low), furnished rooms (bed, table, chair, bookshelf, cupboard), mess with multi-cuisine meals, gymnasium (optional, Rs. 1000/semester), laundry facilities, and common lounges.

question: What is the B.Tech fee at JECRC University?
answer: JECRC University B.Tech fees per year range from Rs. 1,39,000 to Rs. 2,20,000 depending on branch and specialization. Approximate total 4-year cost: CSE core ~Rs. 7-9.5 lakh, CSE with AI/ML or Data Science specialization ~Rs. 9.5-10 lakh. A one-time admission + alumni fee of Rs. 10,000 is charged in the first semester. Hostel, mess, transport, and security deposits are extra. Fees are paid in 2 installments per semester.

question: What is the fee for B.Tech CSE at JECRC University?
answer: B.Tech CSE at JECRC University costs approximately Rs. 1,75,000 to Rs. 2,45,000 per year depending on specialization. Total 4-year cost is approximately Rs. 9.5-10 lakh for CSE with AI/ML or Data Science. For CSE core, it is around Rs. 7-9 lakh for 4 years. A one-time non-refundable admission and alumni fee of Rs. 10,000 is charged in the first semester. For exact current figures, visit jecrcuniversity.edu.in or contact admissions.

question: What is the fee for B.Tech CSE AI ML at JECRC University?
answer: B.Tech CSE with AI and Machine Learning specialization at JECRC University costs approximately Rs. 1,74,000 to Rs. 2,20,000 per year. The 4-year total is approximately Rs. 9.5-10 lakh. This is slightly higher than CSE core due to the specialization. Scholarships are available: 15% tuition waiver for 70-84.99% in Class 12 or JEE Main 85-89.99 percentile, and 20% waiver for 85%+ in Class 12 or JEE Main 90+ percentile (subject to maintaining 8.0 CGPA).

question: What scholarships are available at JECRC University?
answer: JECRC University offers merit scholarships based on Class 12 scores and JEE Main percentile: 15% tuition fee waiver for 70-84.99% in Class 12 or JEE Main 85-89.99 percentile; 20% waiver for 85%+ in Class 12 or JEE Main 90+ percentile. Scholarship requires maintaining 8.0 CGPA and 75% attendance each semester — failing which it is withdrawn. Scholarships apply only to tuition fees; hostel, mess, and other charges are not covered. Government scholarship schemes like those on SSO Rajasthan portal are also applicable.

question: What is the minimum attendance required at JECRC University?
answer: JECRC University requires a minimum of 75% attendance in each subject to be eligible to appear in end semester exams. Students falling below 75% in any subject may be debarred from that subject's exam. Medical leaves require proper documentation submitted to the department. Students can check their attendance on the student ERP portal. Scholarship students must maintain 75% attendance or risk losing their scholarship.

question: How do I check my attendance at JECRC?
answer: Attendance can be checked through the JECRC University student ERP portal. Login with your university-issued credentials. Minimum 75% attendance is required per subject to sit in end semester exams. If your attendance is dropping, contact your class teacher or HOD immediately — there is no relaxation without valid medical documentation submitted on time.

question: What is the placement record of JECRC University?
answer: JECRC University placements 2025: 2,230+ students placed in 140+ companies. Highest CTC was Rs. 33 LPA and average CTC was Rs. 6 LPA. Top recruiters include TCS, Infosys, Wipro, Capgemini, HCL, Accenture, Deloitte, FedEx, HDFC Bank, BYJU's and many more. CSE and its specializations (AI/ML, Data Science) consistently lead in placements. JECRC also received 1030+ placement offers for the 2026 batch in Phase 1 alone within the first 100 days of placement season.

question: How do I register for placements at JECRC?
answer: Placement registrations typically open in the 7th semester for B.Tech students. Register through the Training and Placement Office (TPO) portal or visit the TPO office directly on campus. Keep your resume updated and maintain a good CGPA. Attend all pre-placement training and mock interview sessions organized by the placement cell. Top recruiting companies include TCS, Infosys, Wipro, Capgemini, Accenture, and many startups.

question: What is the exam schedule at JECRC University?
answer: JECRC University exam schedule: End semester exams are held in November-December for odd semester and April-May for even semester. Mid-term exams are held midway through each semester. Back exams (for failed subjects) are conducted after the main exams — register through the examination cell by paying the required fee. Exact timetables are published on jecrcuniversity.edu.in approximately 2-3 weeks before exams.

question: What is the fee submission deadline at JECRC?
answer: JECRC University fees are paid in two installments per academic year. The first installment is due at the start of the odd semester (July-August) and the second installment is due in November. Exact cutoff dates are announced via the official Fee Demand Notice on jecrcuniversity.edu.in. Late fees are charged after the deadline. Check the official notice board or website for current year dates.

question: How do I apply for a scholarship at JECRC University?
answer: JECRC University scholarships are automatically applied based on your Class 12 percentage or JEE Main percentile at the time of admission. No separate application is needed for institutional scholarships — you receive the waiver if eligible. For government scholarships (OBC/SC/ST/minority), apply via the SSO Rajasthan portal (sso.rajasthan.gov.in). Required documents typically include marksheets, income certificate, caste certificate, and bank details. Contact the scholarship cell in the admin block for guidance.

question: How do I apply for leave at JECRC University?
answer: For short leaves, submit a leave application to your class teacher or HOD with the reason stated clearly. For medical leave, submit a valid doctor's certificate within 3 days of returning. For extended leaves, approval from the Dean of Students is required. Always apply before taking leave when possible — retroactive applications may not be accepted. Unexplained absences directly impact your attendance percentage and may put your exam eligibility at risk.

question: How do I get a bonafide or character certificate at JECRC?
answer: Bonafide and character certificates are issued from the university admin office. Submit a written application with your name, enrollment number, branch, year, and purpose of the certificate. Processing takes 2-3 working days. Some certificates are also available through the student ERP portal. Carry your student ID when collecting. Common reasons accepted include bank account opening, scholarship applications, internship applications, and passport applications.

question: What documents are needed for hostel admission at JECRC?
answer: Documents required for JECRC hostel admission: Aadhar card (original + photocopy), admission letter, recent passport-size photographs (4-6), parent/guardian contact details and ID proof, medical fitness certificate, fee payment receipt, and an undertaking that you will stay for the full academic year and pay both installments. Note that hostel fee once paid is fully non-refundable under any circumstances including if you vacate mid-year.

question: What clubs and technical communities are at JECRC University?
answer: JECRC University has numerous active clubs and communities including: IEEE Student Chapter, JECRC Incubation Centre (JIC), Coding Ecosystem Club, JECRC MUN Society, JECRC NSS, NCC, Cultural Clubs, IAESTE Local Committee (for international internships), JU Maker Space, JU Socialz (social media marketing), and Abhyudaya (social service). The university also hosts events like National Healthcare Hackathon and National ISRO Science Exhibition. Club registrations usually open at the start of the academic year.

question: What are the library facilities at JECRC University?
answer: JECRC University has a central library with a large collection of books, journals, and digital resources. Students can borrow books using their student ID card. Library timings are approximately 8 AM to 8 PM on weekdays. Digital/online resources are accessible via the student portal. The library also has reading rooms and computer terminals. For specific resource availability or inter-library loan requests, contact the library directly.

question: What sports facilities are available at JECRC University?
answer: JECRC University has sports facilities including cricket ground, basketball court, badminton court, table tennis, and a gymnasium. The gymnasium is available for hostellers at Rs. 1,000 per semester (optional). Sports teams participate in inter-college tournaments. The university also has an active sports culture with events organized throughout the year. Contact the sports department for detailed timing and team registration information.

question: What is the process for a back or compartment exam at JECRC?
answer: If you fail a subject, you can appear in the back exam (also called compartment or supplementary exam) conducted after the main semester exams. Fill the back exam form (available online on the student portal and offline at the examination cell) and pay the required fee. Back exams are usually held in May-June for even semester and November for odd semester. Clearing backlogs is critical — uncleared backlogs affect your CGPA and may disqualify you from campus placements.

question: How do I apply for re-evaluation of my answer sheet at JECRC?
answer: Apply for re-evaluation within 15 days of result declaration. Get the re-evaluation form from the examination cell, fill it, and submit with the required fee. Results typically take 3-4 weeks. If your re-evaluation score changes, the revised marks are updated in your records. For queries about the current fee and exact process, contact the JECRC examination cell directly.

question: What is the grievance redressal process at JECRC University?
answer: JECRC University has a formal grievance cell. Steps: 1) For academic issues, first approach your class teacher or HOD. 2) If unresolved, approach the Dean of the respective school. 3) For serious grievances, submit a written complaint to the Student Grievance Cell. 4) Hostel complaints can be submitted to the warden or online via the hostel complaint portal. The university also has Anti-Ragging committees — report any ragging incident immediately to the warden, security, or call the national anti-ragging helpline.

question: What are the college timings and academic calendar at JECRC?
answer: JECRC University classes run from 8:30 AM to approximately 3:20-5:00 PM, Monday to Saturday. The academic year has two semesters: July to November (odd semester) and January to May (even semester). The full academic calendar including exam dates, holidays, and event schedules is published on jecrcuniversity.edu.in at the start of each year. A summer semester is also sometimes offered for specific courses or remedial purposes.

question: How do I contact JECRC University administration?
answer: JECRC University is located at: Plot No. IS-2036 to 2039, Ramchandrapura Industrial Area, Vidhani, Sitapura Extension, Jaipur, Rajasthan - 303905. Official website: jecrcuniversity.edu.in. Social media: Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, YouTube (@jecrcuniversity). For admissions: visit the admissions office on campus or fill the online form at jecrcuniversity.edu.in. Admin office hours are approximately 9 AM to 5 PM on working days. For hostel queries, contact the respective warden directly.

question: What is the Wi-Fi facility in JECRC hostel?
answer: JECRC University hostels have Wi-Fi connectivity. However, based on student reviews, the internet speed in hostels has been reported as low (around 2 Mbps), which can be limiting. High-powered electrical appliances are prohibited in rooms. If you need better connectivity for academic work, the university campus labs and library have better facilities. You can also use personal mobile data as a supplement.

question: What are the mess timings at JECRC hostel?
answer: JECRC hostel mess timings: Breakfast 7:30 AM - 8:30 AM (Sunday: 7:30 AM - 9:00 AM), Lunch 11:30 AM - 2:00 PM, Evening Snacks 5:15 PM - 6:30 PM, Dinner 7:30 PM - 9:30 PM. The mess is managed in partnership with Stanza Living and offers multi-cuisine meals in a centrally air-conditioned dining area. Mess quality has had mixed reviews — good initially but varies over time. Mess fee is included in the hostel fee.

question: How do I apply for internship support from JECRC University?
answer: The Training and Placement Office (TPO) at JECRC helps students find internships. Register with the TPO and keep your resume updated. The university also has an IAESTE Local Committee which facilitates international internship opportunities. For independent internships you find yourself, the TPO can provide university documentation and NOC letters. Attend all internship drives and pre-placement training sessions for best results. The Career Development and Guidance Cell (CDGC) also provides career counselling.

question: What is the transportation fee at JECRC University?
answer: JECRC University transportation fee for Academic Year 2025-26 is Rs. 50,000 per year (Rs. 30,000 at the time of admission and Rs. 20,000 in the second semester). This is for university bus/transport service. It is optional — students who prefer to arrange their own transport do not need to pay this. For hostel students, transport is generally not needed as everything is on campus.
